_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Affected skin of the toe is inflamed
Redness, swelling, and pain in the affected area
New tissue starts growing over the toenail
Inflamed area may weep, bleed, or release pus
Unpleasant odour, especially if infected by bacteria

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with history and examination and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with detailed history, signs and symptoms, clinical examination, planned line of treatment, and indications for the procedure?
Were the patient's clinical history/investigations indicative of the Procedure?
Whether the investigation reports confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed Operative/Procedure notes available?
Is the discharge summary with follow-up advice available at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical history or investigations are not indicative of the procedure.
Investigation reports do not confirm the diagnosis of ingrown toenail.
Pre-operative clinical photograph not submitted or does not confirm the diagnosis.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are not available.
Detailed Operative/Procedure notes were not submitted or are incomplete.
Discharge Summary not submitted, incomplete, or lacks follow-up advice.

clinical_key_pointers: Ingrown toenails (onychocryptosis/unguis incarnates) are a common problem, and causes include wearing tight footwear, infection, improperly trimmed toenails, trauma, and heredity. The great toenail is most involved, with the lateral side being more involved than the medical side.
surgical_options: Wedge resection of the toenail and nail fold
Excision of the affected nail and total matricectomy (chemical or mechanical)
Electrocautery, radiofrequency ablation, and carbon dioxide laser ablation
additional_information.referral_criteria: Cases may require referral to an appropriate level of care as required.
